003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.hocon;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022
023/**
024 * Tokenizer for HOCON (Human-Optimized Config Object Notation) format.
025 *
026 * <p>
027 * Handles unquoted strings, quoted strings, triple-quoted strings, numbers, booleans, null,
028 * structural tokens, substitutions ({@code ${var}} and {@code ${?var}}), and comments.
029 *
030 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
031 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://github.com/lightbend/config/blob/main/HOCON.md">HOCON Specification</a>
032 * </ul>
033 */
034@SuppressWarnings({
035   "java:S135",  // Multiple break/continue necessary for tokenizer state machine loops
036   "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for HOCON grammar
037   "resource" // Reader field wraps the caller's reader (BufferedReader/PushbackReader hold only in-memory buffers); the underlying reader is caller-owned.
038})
039public class HoconTokenizer {
040
041   /** Characters that cannot appear in unquoted strings per HOCON spec (includes . for path parsing). Note: / is allowed for paths like /usr/local. */
042   private static final String UNQUOTED_FORBIDDEN = "$\"{}[]:=,+#^\\?!@*&\t\n\r ";
043
044   /** Token types produced by the tokenizer. */
045   public enum TokenType {
046      /** Unquoted string (key or value). */
047      UNQUOTED_STRING,
048      /** Double or single-quoted string. */
049      QUOTED_STRING,
050      /** Triple-quoted string ("""..."""). */
051      TRIPLE_QUOTED,
052      /** Number. */
053      NUMBER,
054      /** Boolean true. */
055      TRUE,
056      /** Boolean false. */
057      FALSE,
058      /** Null value. */
059      NULL,
060      /** Left brace {. */
061      LBRACE,
062      /** Right brace }. */
063      RBRACE,
064      /** Left bracket [. */
065      LBRACKET,
066      /** Right bracket ]. */
067      RBRACKET,
068      /** Colon :. */
069      COLON,
070      /** Equals =. */
071      EQUALS,
072      /** Plus-equals +=. */
073      PLUS_EQUALS,
074      /** Comma ,. */
075      COMMA,
076      /** Significant newline (member separator). */
077      NEWLINE,
078      /** Substitution ${var}. */
079      SUBSTITUTION,
080      /** Optional substitution ${?var}. */
081      OPT_SUBSTITUTION,
082      /** End of input. */
083      EOF
084   }
085
086   /**
087    * A token with optional string or number value.
088    *
089    * @param type Token type.
090    * @param stringValue String value for string tokens; <jk>null</jk> otherwise.
091    * @param numberValue Number value for NUMBER tokens; <jk>null</jk> otherwise.
092    */
093   public record Token(TokenType type, String stringValue, Number numberValue) {
094      /**
095       * Creates a structural or literal token with no value.
096       *
097       * @param type Token type.
098       * @return A new token.
099       */
100      public static Token of(TokenType type) {
101         return new Token(type, null, null);
102      }
103      /**
104       * Creates a string token.
105       *
106       * @param value The string value.
107       * @return A new token.
108       */
109      public static Token string(String value) {
110         return new Token(TokenType.UNQUOTED_STRING, value, null);
111      }
112      /**
113       * Creates a quoted-string token.
114       *
115       * @param value The string value.
116       * @return A new token.
117       */
118      public static Token quoted(String value) {
119         return new Token(TokenType.QUOTED_STRING, value, null);
120      }
121      /**
122       * Creates a triple-quoted string token.
123       *
124       * @param value The string value.
125       * @return A new token.
126       */
127      public static Token tripleQuoted(String value) {
128         return new Token(TokenType.TRIPLE_QUOTED, value, null);
129      }
130      /**
131       * Creates a number token.
132       *
133       * @param value The number value.
134       * @return A new token.
135       */
136      public static Token number(Number value) {
137         return new Token(TokenType.NUMBER, null, value);
138      }
139      /**
140       * Creates a substitution token.
141       *
142       * @param path The reference path.
143       * @param optional Whether it is optional (${?var}).
144       * @return A new token.
145       */
146      public static Token substitution(String path, boolean optional) {
147         return new Token(optional ? TokenType.OPT_SUBSTITUTION : TokenType.SUBSTITUTION, path, null);
148      }
149   }
150
151   private final PushbackReader reader;
152   private Token peeked;
153   private int line = 1;
154   private int column = 1;
155
156   /**
157    * Constructor.
158    *
159    * @param reader The reader to tokenize.
160    */
161   public HoconTokenizer(Reader reader) {
162      var in = reader instanceof BufferedReader br ? br : new BufferedReader(reader);
163      this.reader = new PushbackReader(in, 8);
164   }
165
166   /**
167    * Returns the current line number (1-based).
168    *
169    * @return The line number.
170    */
171   public int getLine() {
172      return line;
173   }
174
175   /**
176    * Returns the current column number (1-based).
177    *
178    * @return The column number.
179    */
180   public int getColumn() {
181      return column;
182   }
183
184   /**
185    * Peeks at the next token without consuming it.
186    *
187    * @return The next token.
188    * @throws IOException If a read error occurs.
189    */
190   public Token peek() throws IOException {
191      if (peeked == null)
192         peeked = readToken();
193      return peeked;
194   }
195
196   /**
197    * Peeks at the next token without skipping whitespace first.
198    * Caller must call {@link #skipWhitespaceAndCommentsExceptNewlines()} first when checking for value concatenation.
199    *
200    * @return The next token.
201    * @throws IOException If a read error occurs.
202    */
203   public Token peekNoSkip() throws IOException {
204      if (peeked == null)
205         peeked = readTokenImmediate();
206      return peeked;
207   }
208
209   /**
210    * Reads and consumes the next token.
211    *
212    * @return The next token.
213    * @throws IOException If a read error occurs.
214    */
215   public Token read() throws IOException {
216      if (peeked != null) {
217         var t = peeked;
218         peeked = null;
219         return t;
220      }
221      return readToken();
222   }
223
224   private int readChar() throws IOException {
225      var c = reader.read();
226      if (c == '\n' || (c == '\r' && peekChar() != '\n')) {
227         line++;
228         column = 1;
229      } else if (c >= 0) {
230         column++;
231      }
232      return c;
233   }
234
235   private int peekChar() throws IOException {
236      var c = reader.read();
237      if (c >= 0)
238         reader.unread(c);
239      return c;
240   }
241
242   private void unread(int c) throws IOException {
243      if (c >= 0) {
244         reader.unread(c);
245         column--;
246         if (c == '\n')
247            line--;
248      }
249   }
250
251   /**
252    * Skips whitespace and comments.
253    *
254    * <p>
255    * No-op when a token has already been peeked (i.e. {@link #peek()} or {@link #peekNoSkip()}
256    * cached a token). Once a token is cached, the underlying reader is positioned <em>after</em>
257    * that token, so skipping whitespace here would consume characters that semantically come after
258    * the cached token rather than before it. The leading whitespace before the cached token was
259    * already consumed when the token was produced, so this method has nothing left to do until the
260    * cached token is read. This invariant keeps callers from accidentally reading past a peeked
261    * structural token (such as a closing {@code ]} or {@code }}) and swallowing a separator that
262    * belongs to an enclosing scope.
263    *
264    * @throws IOException If a read error occurs.
265    */
266   public void skipWhitespaceAndComments() throws IOException {
267      if (peeked != null)
268         return;
269      while (true) {
270         var c = readChar();
271         if (c < 0)
272            return;
273         if (c == ' ' || c == '\t' || c == '\r' || c == '\n')
274            continue;
275         if (c == '#') {
276            skipLineComment();
277            continue;
278         }
279         if (c == '/' && peekChar() == '/') {
280            readChar();
281            skipLineComment();
282            continue;
283         }
284         unread(c);
285         return;
286      }
287   }
288
289   private void skipLineComment() throws IOException {
290      var c = readChar();
291      while (c >= 0 && c != '\n' && c != '\r')
292         c = readChar();
293      if (c == '\r' && peekChar() == '\n')
294         readChar();
295   }
296
297   /** Skips a line comment but stops before the newline (does not consume it). */
298   private void skipLineCommentExceptNewline() throws IOException {
299      var c = readChar();
300      while (c >= 0 && c != '\n' && c != '\r') {
301         c = readChar();
302      }
303      if (c >= 0)
304         unread(c);
305   }
306
307   /**
308    * Skips whitespace and comments but stops at newlines.
309    * Used when parsing value concatenation so newlines end the value.
310    *
311    * @throws IOException If a read error occurs.
312    */
313   public void skipWhitespaceAndCommentsExceptNewlines() throws IOException {
314      while (true) {
315         var c = readChar();
316         if (c < 0)
317            return;
318         if (c == '\n' || c == '\r') {
319            unread(c);
320            return;
321         }
322         if (c == ' ' || c == '\t')
323            continue;
324         if (c == '#') {
325            skipLineCommentExceptNewline();
326            continue;
327         }
328         if (c == '/' && peekChar() == '/') {
329            readChar();
330            skipLineCommentExceptNewline();
331            continue;
332         }
333         unread(c);
334         return;
335      }
336   }
337
338   private Token readToken() throws IOException {
339      skipWhitespaceAndComments();
340      return readTokenImmediate();
341   }
342
343   private Token readTokenImmediate() throws IOException {
344      var c = readChar();
345      if (c < 0)
346         return Token.of(TokenType.EOF);
347
348      if (c == '{') return Token.of(TokenType.LBRACE);
349      if (c == '}') return Token.of(TokenType.RBRACE);
350      if (c == '[') return Token.of(TokenType.LBRACKET);
351      if (c == ']') return Token.of(TokenType.RBRACKET);
352      if (c == ',') return Token.of(TokenType.COMMA);
353      if (c == '\n' || c == '\r') {
354         if (c == '\r' && peekChar() == '\n')
355            readChar();
356         return Token.of(TokenType.NEWLINE);
357      }
358
359      if (c == ':') {
360         return Token.of(TokenType.COLON);
361      }
362      // An equals sign always produces the EQUALS token regardless of the character that follows it.
363      // The plus-equals operator is recognized by the separate check below that reads the leading
364      // plus first, so an equals immediately followed by a plus is never mistaken for a partial
365      // plus-equals operator.
366      if (c == '=')
367         return Token.of(TokenType.EQUALS);
368      if (c == '+' && peekChar() == '=') {
369         readChar();
370         return Token.of(TokenType.PLUS_EQUALS);
371      }
372
373      if (c == '"') {
374         if (peekChar() == '"' && peekChar(2) == '"') {
375            readChar();
376            readChar();
377            return Token.tripleQuoted(readTripleQuotedString());
378         }
379         unread(c);
380         return Token.quoted(readQuotedString());
381      }
382      if (c == '\'') {
383         unread(c);
384         return Token.quoted(readQuotedString());
385      }
386
387      if (c == '$' && peekChar() == '{') {
388         readChar();
389         return readSubstitutionToken();
390      }
391
392      unread(c);
393      return readUnquotedOrNumber();
394   }
395
396   private Token readSubstitutionToken() throws IOException {
397      var sb = new StringBuilder();
398      var c = readChar();
399      var optional = c == '?';
400      if (optional)
401         c = readChar();
402      while (c >= 0 && c != '}') {
403         if (c == ' ' || c == '\t' || c == '\n' || c == '\r')
404            break;
405         sb.append((char) c);
406         c = readChar();
407      }
408      if (c == '}')
409         return Token.substitution(sb.toString().trim(), optional);
410      // Malformed ${...} - treat as string
411      return Token.string("${" + (optional ? "?" : "") + sb);
412   }
413
414   private Token readUnquotedOrNumber() throws IOException {
415      var sb = new StringBuilder();
416      var c = readChar();
417      while (c >= 0 && UNQUOTED_FORBIDDEN.indexOf(c) < 0) {
418         sb.append((char) c);
419         c = readChar();
420      }
421      if (c >= 0)
422         unread(c);
423      var raw = sb.toString().trim();
424      // Guard against infinite recursion: if we haven't consumed any chars and the next char is
425      // a forbidden one that wasn't handled as a structural / operator token by readTokenImmediate
426      // (e.g. orphan `+`, `$`, or `=` not followed by their expected continuation), we'd loop
427      // forever calling back into readToken().  Surface this as a clean IOException instead so the
428      // parser converts it to a ParseException rather than a StackOverflowError.
429      if (raw.isEmpty()) {
430         if (c >= 0)
431            throw new IOException("Unexpected character '" + (char) c + "' at line " + line + ", column " + column);
432         return Token.of(TokenType.EOF);
433      }
434
435      if (raw.equals("true")) return Token.of(TokenType.TRUE);
436      if (raw.equals("false")) return Token.of(TokenType.FALSE);
437      if (raw.equals("null")) return Token.of(TokenType.NULL);
438
439      // Whole-string JSON-number match (leading-zero-restricted grammar); span equals the full token on a match.
440      if (matchNumberPrefix(raw, false) == raw.length()) {
441         var numStr = raw;
442         try {
443            if (numStr.contains(".") || numStr.toLowerCase().contains("e"))
444               return Token.number(Double.parseDouble(numStr));
445            return parseIntegerOrLongToken(numStr);
446         } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
447            // Number too large for Long or other parse failure; fall through to unquoted
448         }
449      }
450
451      return Token.string(raw);
452   }
453
454   private static Token parseIntegerOrLongToken(String numStr) {
455      try {
456         return Token.number(Integer.parseInt(numStr));
457      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") NumberFormatException e) {
458         return Token.number(Long.parseLong(numStr));
459      }
460   }
461
462   private int peekChar(int offset) throws IOException {
463      var buf = new int[offset];
464      for (var i = 0; i < offset; i++)
465         buf[i] = readChar();
466      for (var i = offset - 1; i >= 0; i--)
467         unread(buf[i]);
468      return offset > 0 ? buf[offset - 1] : -1;
469   }
470
471   /**
472    * Reads a quoted string ("..." or '...') with JSON-style escaping.
473    *
474    * @return The string value.
475    * @throws IOException If a read error occurs.
476    */
477   public String readQuotedString() throws IOException {
478      var quote = readChar();
479      if (quote != '"' && quote != '\'')
480         return "";
481      var sb = new StringBuilder();
482      var c = readChar();
483      while (c >= 0 && c != quote) {
484         if (c == '\\') {
485            c = readChar();
486            if (c >= 0) {
487               if (c == 'u')
488                  sb.append(readUnicodeEscape());
489               else
490                  sb.append(unescape(c));
491            }
492         } else {
493            sb.append((char) c);
494         }
495         c = readChar();
496      }
497      return sb.toString();
498   }
499
500   private static char unescape(int c) {
501      return switch (c) {
502         case '"' -> '"';
503         case '\'' -> '\'';
504         case '\\' -> '\\';
505         case '/' -> '/';
506         case 'b' -> '\b';
507         case 'f' -> '\f';
508         case 'n' -> '\n';
509         case 'r' -> '\r';
510         case 't' -> '\t';
511         default -> (char) c;
512      };
513   }
514
515   private char readUnicodeEscape() throws IOException {
516      var hex = new StringBuilder(4);
517      for (var i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
518         var c = readChar();
519         if (c < 0 || !isHexDigit(c))
520            throw new IOException("Invalid \\u escape sequence at line " + line + ", column " + column);
521         hex.append((char) c);
522      }
523      return (char) Integer.parseInt(hex.toString(), 16);
524   }
525
526   private static boolean isHexDigit(int c) {
527      return (c >= '0' && c <= '9') || (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f') || (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F');
528   }
529
530   /**
531    * Reads an unquoted string until a forbidden character.
532    *
533    * @return The string value.
534    * @throws IOException If a read error occurs.
535    */
536   public String readUnquotedString() throws IOException {
537      var sb = new StringBuilder();
538      var c = readChar();
539      while (c >= 0 && UNQUOTED_FORBIDDEN.indexOf(c) < 0) {
540         sb.append((char) c);
541         c = readChar();
542      }
543      if (c >= 0)
544         unread(c);
545      return sb.toString().trim();
546   }
547
548   /**
549    * Reads a triple-quoted string ("""..."""). No escape processing inside per HOCON spec.
550    *
551    * @return The string value.
552    * @throws IOException If a read error occurs.
553    */
554   public String readTripleQuotedString() throws IOException {
555      var sb = new StringBuilder();
556      var quoteCount = 0;
557      var c = readChar();
558      while (c >= 0) {
559         if (c == '"') {
560            quoteCount++;
561            if (quoteCount == 3) {
562               var s = sb.toString();
563               if (s.endsWith("\n"))
564                  s = s.substring(0, s.length() - 1);
565               return s.replace("\r\n", "\n").replace("\r", "\n");
566            }
567         } else {
568            if (quoteCount == 1)
569               sb.append('"');
570            else if (quoteCount == 2)
571               sb.append("\"\"");
572            quoteCount = 0;
573            if (c == '\r' && peekChar() == '\n') {
574               readChar();
575               sb.append('\n');
576            } else if (c != '\r') {
577               sb.append((char) c);
578            }
579         }
580         c = readChar();
581      }
582      return sb.toString();
583   }
584}
